purpose: Twelfth AS-owned schematic-linked Universal Cell trace, covering recipient-side rejection of two simultaneous command-message tokens.

ports.label: north
ports.summary: First signal channel carrying the wire-r-init command-message token.

ports.label: east
ports.summary: Second signal channel carrying the proc-l-init command-message token.

ports.label: west
ports.summary: Third signal channel, empty during this multi-command rejection trace.

layers.summary: Two simultaneous command-message tokens on a recipient fixed cell are rejected rather than prioritized or sequenced.
layers.boundary: This layer states the ADR-0059 multi-command rejection policy only; it does not widen command execution.

layers.summary: The multi-command rejection node uses the same triangular GELC/RLEM port vocabulary as the other traces.
layers.boundary: This layer fixes schematic geometry and port orientation, not a complete multi-cell command-routing circuit.

layers.summary: The trace records every current AS Cell field before and after one direct multi-command recipient rejection.
layers.boundary: This layer is limited to the ADR-0059 reject-and-clear policy and adds no priority or sequencing semantics.

layers.summary: Switchable circulator hardware and auxiliary command-routing support remain candidate interpretations.
layers.boundary: This layer keeps physical implementation and RALA-like auxiliary circuitry separate from the tested rejection transition.

routed_signal_flow: command[wire-r-init+proc-l-init] rejected as recipient non-init command-message
